channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump reportedly plans to unleash around 100 executive orders after taking office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump reportedly signaled that he plans to push around 100 executive orders once he takes office later in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California's working class overlooked amid wildfires, US rep says
item.description: Rep. Maxine Waters, D-Calif., called for attention to the working class people in Altadena, California, who have “limited resources" amid the wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Schools nationwide brace for Trump, including measures compelling teachers not to cooperate with ICE
item.description: K-12 school districts, universities and state-level education leaders in the U.S. are drawing up plans to protect students from Trump's immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state by inserting Bible references into reading lessons and requiring teachers to post the Ten Commandments.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Winter weather limits North Carolina governor's inauguration to TV speech
item.description: North Carolina's new governor is planning to give his inauguration speech indoors in a televised address after the outdoor public ceremony was canceled due to forecasts for snow, sleet and freezing rain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis open to special session for immigration, hurricane relief
item.description: It’s widely known that Gov. Ron DeSantis is open to the idea of a special session to help resolve the condo affordability crisis. However, the governor is also open to the idea of state lawmakers doing more before the yearly scheduled session starts.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fills latest Cabinet spots as Jan. 20 inauguration nears
item.description: President-elect Trump named Bill Briggs as Deputy Administrator of the U.S. Small Business Administration and Ed Russo as his pick for the Environmental Advisory Task Force.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden is still considering pardons for people who have been criticized or threatened by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Friday said he was still considering whether to give pardons to people who have been criticized or threatened by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wife of American detained in Afghanistan heads to Mar-a-Lago to beg Trump to take up prisoner swap
item.description: A wife desperate to bring her husband home after two-plus years of wrongful detainment in Afghanistan has flown to Mar-a-Lago to implore President-elect Trump to take up her case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Legislature prepares for new session, rifts between parties show a partisan divide
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature will gavel-in a new session on Jan. 14 at noon, and MPR’s politics team is at the Capitol to cover it all. Coming up Monday at 9 a.m., MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Judge holds Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for continued lies about Georgia election workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court Friday for the second time in a week, as a federal judge warned him he could be sent to jail if he doesn't stop spreading lies about two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgement against him. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI must be independent and above the partisan fray, outgoing director says in farewell address
item.description: Outgoing FBI Director Christopher Wray says the bureau must remain independent, above the partisan fray and “committed to upholding the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump vows to shed light on mystery drones when president
item.description: Trump, who will be inaugurated as the 47th president on Jan. 20, criticized the Biden administration's handling of the mystery drones.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump can still vote after sentencing, but can't own a gun and will have to turn over DNA sample
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump doesn't have to go to jail, pay a fine or perform community service as a result of his New York hush money conviction. A judge ended the case Friday with a sentence of an unconditional discharge, closing the case with no punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans blast 'joke' sentencing of Trump 10 days before swearing in
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced for falsifying records just 10 days before he is set to take office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge sentences Trump to unconditional discharge, no punishment in hush money conviction
item.description: President-elect Trump was spared any punishment for his hush money criminal conviction Friday when a New York judge sentenced him to an unconditional discharge, eschewing jail time while securing his status as the first person convicted of a felony to assume the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President-elect Trump appears virtually in court to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is appearing virtually in a New York courtroom to be sentenced for his hush money conviction, 10 days before he is inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump is sentenced in hush money case
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump received an unconditional discharge for his criminal conviction, meaning he will get a criminal record but no other penalties.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Freedom Caucus members to huddle with Trump on strategy
item.description: Members of the House Freedom Caucus say they will make the case for their preferred policies and strategy when they meet with President-elect Trump at Mar-a-Lago on Friday to discuss his ambitious legislative agenda — bringing preferences that at times clash with those of Trump. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to deliver a capstone address on his foreign policy legacy as he makes way for Trump's return
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden plans to deliver a capstone address Monday on his foreign policy legacy, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remembering President Jimmy Carter: Not your stereotypical politician
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Jimmy Carter was not your stereotypical politician. He was a deeply religious man and more of a moral leader than a political one.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, PIX 11

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral brings together 5 current and former US presidents to honor one of their own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As they filed into the front pews at Washington National Cathedral, wearing dark suits and mostly solemn faces, five current and former presidents came together for Jimmy Carter's funeral.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court refuses Trump’s request to block Friday’s criminal sentencing
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 decision on Thursday refused President-elect Trump’s request to halt his criminal sentencing, now just hours away, closing off Trump’s last remaining pathway to avoid receiving his punishment for his 34-count felony conviction in New York.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: The Memo: Trump-Newsom feud ramps up as Los Angeles fires rage on
item.description: The feud between President-elect Trump and California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) is hitting new peaks of intensity as wildfires rage on in Los Angeles. Trump has called on Newsom to resign, while the governor has hit back at Trump for politicizing the disaster.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Obama exchange pleasantries at Carter funeral after long-standing rivalry
item.description: President-elect Trump and former President Obama had an extended friendly exchange as they sat next to each other at the funeral services for former President Carter on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams delivers 4th State of the City address
item.description: HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his fourth State of the City address at The Apollo in Harlem on Thursday. Adams discussed topics like public safety, the cost of living, and new initiatives to build more in the city.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: House Republican bill would pave way for Trump to acquire Panama Canal for US
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson (R-S.D.) will introduce a bill that would authorize the president to purchase the Panama Canal and put it under U.S. control, an acquisition that President-elect Trump has been pushing over the last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: National Day of Mourning: What's closed as Jimmy Carter is laid to rest
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some facilities and operations will be closed on Thursday in observance of the National Day of Mourning following former President Jimmy Carter's death. Here's what's closed: All flags will continue to be flown at half-staff for 30 days from Dec.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: What's next for Canada? 5 questions will help decide the future after Trudeau
item.description: "We're in a unique situation in Canadian politics," Shachi Kurl, president of the Angus Reid Institute, tells NPR. Voters need to replace a lame-duck leader — and cope with threats from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: President-elect Trump asks Supreme Court to block sentencing in hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New California law aims to prevent book bans in libraries statewide
item.description: By 2026, libraries in San Diego must come up with a collection development policy that would outline how they choose what books they'll add to their shelves, as well as provide a way for readers to express their concerns about the library materials and giving them a way to request new ones.
item.dc:creator: Dominique LaVigne, Fox 5

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
